files.Title: FastMoq.Core.Providers.MockWrapper<T>
files.Summary:
Provider-agnostic wrapper around an supplying:

  • Instance (test subject dependency)
  • NativeMock (legacy underlying mock if the provider exposes one)
  • Verify delegation through
  • Transitional Setup helpers (Moq pass-through). Other providers throw until a common Setup abstraction exists.

files.Title: FastMoq.Extensions.MockerHttpMoqExtensions
files.Summary: Moq-specific HTTP setup compatibility helpers that remain available from the provider package. These helpers intentionally stay in the FastMoq.Extensions namespace for low-churn migration, while new tests prefer the provider-neutral HTTP helpers in FastMoq.Core.

files.Summary: Per-call overrides for constructor selection and optional-parameter resolution during instance creation. preserves the current defaults.

files.Summary: Describes custom behavior for a known type handled specially by . Registrations can supply direct instances, managed instances, mock configuration, and post-creation defaults.

files.Summary: Mutable container for the enabled on a instance.

files.Summary: Options that control tracked mock retrieval and creation.

files.Summary: Core mock coordinator (provider-based creation integrated).

files.Title: FastMoq.MockerPolicyOptions
files.Summary: Groups the default policy settings that shape how a resolves built-ins and creates mocks. Per-call options may still override the relevant creation or invocation behavior where supported.

files.Title: FastMoq.MockerTestBase<TComponent>
files.Summary: Mock Test Base with Automatic Mocking using . This class contains the property to create and track all Mocks from creation of the component.

files.Summary: Non generic abstraction for a created mock instance. Prefer and for provider-first flows, and treat as the provider-native escape hatch.

files.Summary: Abstraction implemented by concrete mocking libraries (Moq, NSubstitute, etc.).

files.Summary: Describes optional features a concrete mocking provider supports so the framework can adapt behavior.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.MoqProvider.IFastMockMoqExtensions
files.Summary: Moq-specific convenience extensions for and . These stay in the provider package so the core abstractions remain provider agnostic. Prefer provider-first members such as , , and FastMoq verification helpers first, and use these extensions when the test intentionally needs Moq-specific APIs.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.MoqProvider.MoqLoggerCompatibility
files.Summary: Provides Moq-based logger verification and callback helpers used by the compatibility layer.

files.Summary: Wraps a typed Moq mock in the provider-neutral FastMoq abstraction.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.MoqProvider.MoqMockAdapter
files.Summary: Wraps a non-generic Moq mock in the provider-neutral FastMoq abstraction.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.MoqProvider.MoqMockingProvider
files.Summary: Provider implementation that adapts Moq to the provider-neutral FastMoq abstractions.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.NSubstituteProvider.IFastMockNSubstituteExtensions
files.Summary: NSubstitute-specific convenience extensions for . These stay in the provider package so the core abstractions remain provider agnostic. Prefer provider-first members such as , , and FastMoq verification helpers first, and use these extensions when the test intentionally needs NSubstitute-specific APIs.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.NSubstituteProvider.NSubstituteMockingProvider
files.Summary: Provider implementation that adapts NSubstitute to the provider-neutral FastMoq abstractions.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.ProviderSelectionDiagnostics
files.Summary: Builds consistent provider-selection guidance for provider-specific FastMoq APIs. This keeps runtime messages aligned across the compatibility surface and provider-specific escape hatches.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.TimesSpec
files.Summary: Provider agnostic verification specification. Uses a single verification mode with an optional count where applicable.

files.Title: FastMoq.Providers.TimesSpecMode
